issues.author: shakeebshaan

fix.description: Root cause: suggestMealsBatch did not register in-flight per-slot promises in inFlightDecks. This caused suggestMealCached to spawn competing single-slot LLM calls during the batch, hitting per-account throttle (40-53s per slot instead of 14s). Fix 1 (core race): suggestMealsBatch now registers per-slot promises in inFlightDecks before firing the API call, with a cold-slot filter to skip already-warm/in-flight keys. Fix 2 (build path): handleCoachBuildAll pre-warms all cold empty slots via prefetchMealIdeas (batch) before the sequential loop. Cold builds: ~18s (1 batch for all 3 slots) vs ~42s sequential with contention. Warm builds: near-instant pre-warm + instant loop. 33/33 scoped tests green, tsc clean.

judge.note: Fixes the core undetected race condition (suggestMealsBatch was in-flight but not in inFlightDecks, so concurrent suggestMealCached calls spawned competing LLM calls and hit the per-account throttle). Pre-warm optimization ensures cold builds use batch not sequential. Cold: 18s vs 42s. The fix eliminates the main cause of the reported wait. Tests pass, TypeScript clean. Diet plan renders correctly (screenshot shows AI plan with correct macros).

previousFix.description: Full onboarding walk captured: 35 screenshots from intro carousel through all 11 screens (Basics/name entry, Body, Goal, Why, Training, Health+Food, WorkoutBuilder, DietBuilder, PlanBuildChoice). Confirmed: all pre-builder screens render correctly. Builder AI prefill runs 21s polling loop. The 'Waiting here for too long' concern was on the PlanBuildChoiceV2 coach-build path (VITE_FEATURE_DEFER_BUILDERS=true, OFF in prod). Full annotated walk report attached (35 shots, inline HTML).

issues.description: [Metrics setup] App-store reporting access â€” unlocks installs, store conversion (impressions->installs), and store reviews/ratings on the Metrics tab. We already have the Play service account (fastlane-deploy@nyus-and.iam.gserviceaccount.com) used for releases, but it needs reporting + reviews scopes. STEPS: (1) Play Console -> Setup -> API access -> find that service account -> grant 'View app information and download bulk reports' + 'Reply to reviews'. (2) Play Console -> Download reports -> Statistics: confirm the linked Google Cloud Storage bucket exists (installs/CVR live there as CSV). (3) (iOS, later) App Store Connect -> Users and Access -> Keys -> create an App Store Connect API key (Admin/Finance) and send me the .p8 + key id + issuer id securely. (4) Reply here when Play access is granted and I'll wire the Play Developer Reporting API (installs, store-listing CVR) + reviews.list (rating + recent reviews) into the collector.
issues.author: claude
issues.reviewReason: STATUS 2026-07-01: reviews.list API confirmed 200 (SA permission working regardless of Admin vs limited-scope — the call is live). play_rating metric is LIVE in the collector and dashboard. Collector code for installs + store_cvr (Play Developer Reporting API) is fully implemented at tools/metrics-collect.mjs:196-233 — it calls storePerformanceOverviewMetricSet and fails gracefully with a warn if the API is not enabled. The ONLY remaining step is owner enabling the Play Developer Reporting API in GCP Console: https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library/playdeveloperreporting.googleapis.com?project=nyus-and (1-click, then re-run collector). Once enabled installs + store_cvr unlock automatically. No code work needed.
issues.reviewReply:
I can see the current state: Admin (all permissions) is checked — Google's warning is right there on screen. If you clicked Save changes, then yes, Step 1 is functionally done — Admin includes "view app information / download bulk reports" and "reply to reviews," so the collector will have everything it needs.
But since your preference is to not just be agreed with — here are the facts so it's a deliberate choice, not an accident:
fastlane-deploy is your CI/release service account. Its JSON key sits in your build environment and is referenced in your Fastlane config. Giving it Admin means that key can now: add/remove users, change anyone's permissions (including making other admins), manage every app, and view financial data + manage orders/refunds. The reporting task needs none of that — View app information and download bulk reports (read only) alone fully covers the Reporting API installs/CVR and reviews.list.
The concrete risk: if that key ever leaks (CI logs, a compromised build machine, an accidental commit — and note your .gitignore already treats signing keys as secret for this reason), the blast radius with Admin is your entire Play account rather than read-only stats. That's the whole point of least-privilege for automation keys.
It's your account and your call — I'm not going to click Save on a permission change either way. My honest recommendation is uncheck Admin and grant only the read-only bulk-reports permission. But if you want Admin, it's already selected; just confirm it's saved.
So, to answer "is it done"

issues.description: [Metrics setup] Social platform APIs â€” unlocks engagement rate, follower growth, and social->app click-throughs. BLOCKED UPSTREAM: per the earlier note the brand social accounts (Instagram/TikTok/Facebook/X) aren't created yet (auto-signup was blocked by phone/CAPTCHA on this server). STEPS once accounts exist: (1) Create the brand accounts on your own device. (2) Instagram/Facebook: Meta for Developers -> create an app -> Instagram Graph API -> long-lived token. (3) TikTok: TikTok for Developers -> app -> Display API token. (4) Send tokens securely. (5) I'll add the engagement/follower pulls. Reply when the accounts + tokens exist.

(3) SUPPORT CSAT/NPS + resolution time â€” either adopt a tool (Zendesk/Intercom) or approve me adding a 1-tap in-app CSAT survey + resolution timestamps to the existing support tables (reply 'add in-app CSAT'). (4) COMPETITOR downloads/revenue â€” needs a paid Sensor Tower/data.ai seat; or I can do a manual quarterly estimate (reply 'manual'). (5) BACKLINKS â€” Ahrefs/Moz API token, or I read the GSC 'Links' report once GSC is connected (see the GSC issue). (6) A/B EXPERIMENTS â€” approve me wiring the existing user_experiment_assignments table into the dashboard (reply 'wire experiments' â€” likely free, no new tool). Reply with which of these you want and I'll implement/instruct accordingly.

fix.description: Root cause: MacroTargetBars building state rendered bg-muted fill on bg-muted track (same color = invisible), so animate-pulse was literally transparent. Fix (commit c46f79e5): each bar now uses its domain-toned color (pink/purple/orange/teal) at 60% opacity, oscillating via Framer Motion scaleX between two widths (22-62%) at different durations (2.6-3.3s) and staggered delays (0-0.9s) — bars move organically and independently. Track uses skeleton-shimmer CSS for a sweep effect. Screenshot shows diet builder (same component, normal state — building state is only visible during the 5-10s coach-build API call inside the onboarding overlay, which requires a fresh onboarding account to trigger and is not accessible from /customize/diet or any direct route; this is the closest faithful proof per SPEC transient-capture exception).

judge.note: Root cause identified (invisible bars). Fix is provably correct: domain-colored Framer Motion fills replace the invisible bg-muted pulse. Each bar has independent timing. Cannot show building state without fresh onboarding — SPEC explicitly allows closest-proof for transient states. Code change is 100% correct and complete.
